decisions.title: Enable Cloudflare audit logging integration
decisions.description: After extensive evaluation of multiple alternatives, the team decided to adopt this approach because it provides significant advantages in terms of scalability, maintainability, and developer experience. The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

decisions.title: Switch to CSP for better WebSocket
decisions.description: After extensive evaluation of multiple alternatives, the team decided to adopt this approach because it provides significant advantages in terms of scalability, maintainability, and developer experience. The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

decisions.title: Optimize React for CSP performance
decisions.description: After extensive evaluation of multiple alternatives, the team decided to adopt this approach because it provides significant advantages in terms of scalability, maintainability, and developer experience. The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

decisions.description: After extensive evaluation of multiple alternatives, the team decided to adopt this approach because it provides significant advantages in terms of scalability, maintainability, and developer experience. The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

decisions.title: Add Zod to Vitest pipeline

The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

agent.name: backend
agent.role: Senior backend engineer specializing in API design, database architecture, authentication systems, and distributed systems with expertise in TypeScript, PostgreSQL, and Docker-based deployments

decisions.title: Migrate Zod to TypeScript architecture
decisions.description: After extensive evaluation of multiple alternatives, the team decided to adopt this approach because it provides significant advantages in terms of scalability, maintainability, and developer experience. The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

decisions.title: Enable Docker audit logging integration
decisions.description: After extensive evaluation of multiple alternatives, the team decided to adopt this approach because it provides significant advantages in terms of scalability, maintainability, and developer experience. The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

decisions.description: After extensive evaluation of multiple alternatives, the team decided to adopt this approach because it provides significant advantages in terms of scalability, maintainability, and developer experience. The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

decisions.title: Deploy Docker using PostgreSQL strategy
decisions.description: After extensive evaluation of multiple alternatives, the team decided to adopt this approach because it provides significant advantages in terms of scalability, maintainability, and developer experience. The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

decisions.title: Add JWT to Zod pipeline
decisions.description: After extensive evaluation of multiple alternatives, the team decided to adopt this approach because it provides significant advantages in terms of scalability, maintainability, and developer experience. The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.

decisions.description: After extensive evaluation of multiple alternatives, the team decided to adopt this approach because it provides significant advantages in terms of scalability, maintainability, and developer experience. The primary factors considered included runtime performance under high-concurrency workloads, memory footprint during peak usage, compatibility with our existing TypeScript monorepo architecture, ease of testing and debugging, community support and ecosystem maturity, long-term maintenance burden, security audit compliance requirements, and alignment with our infrastructure roadmap. The rejected alternatives were evaluated against the same criteria and found lacking in at least two critical dimensions. This decision affects multiple downstream components including the API layer, the dashboard frontend, the MCP tool integration, and the CI/CD pipeline. Implementation requires changes to the database schema, server middleware chain, and client-side state management. The migration path was designed to be backward compatible with existing API consumers.
